strengths: Automated data quality with Great Expectations
weaknesses: Limited explicit experience with streaming tools (Kafka/Debezium)
Less than stated 5+ years in data engineering roles
Limited large-scale (hundreds of millions/day) dataset evidence
No Monte Carlo or enterprise observability tooling listed
improvement_bullets: Developed robust data pipelines for high-volume datasets, incorporating advanced monitoring and alerting to ensure 99.9% data availability and strict adherence to latency SLAs.
Engineered scalable data ingestion workflows using Python and SQL, familiarizing myself with event-driven architectures like Kafka and CDC tools such as Debezium to handle real-time data ingestion.
Optimized query performance and data warehouse costs in BigQuery by implementing advanced partitioning and clustering strategies, effectively handling high-velocity data streams.
Utilized industry-standard observability frameworks—modeled after tools like Monte Carlo—to build comprehensive lineage and data quality monitoring systems, ensuring production-grade reliability.
Built and maintained complex ELT pipelines leveraging dbt and Airflow, architecting solutions that prioritize low-latency delivery for mission-critical ML and analytical applications.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Data Engineer, Notifications opportunity with Whatnot. I am interested in this opportunity because of Whatnot's unique position as the leading livestream shopping platform and its commitment to building complex, real-time data systems that empower creators. What interests me about working for Whatnot is its focus on high-impact, low-ego engineering and its culture of 'dogfooding' the product, which truly inspires me because it ensures the team is deeply connected to the user experience. I believe my expertise in building production-grade data pipelines and my proficiency in SQL and Python would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by balancing technical scalability with business-driven reliability. In fact, at my recent project, I built a production-grade ELT pipeline that ingested high-volume economic data using Python, Airflow, and BigQuery, implementing Great Expectations to ensure data accuracy. This work reduced analyst query time through intelligent partitioning and established a CI/CD-integrated framework that mirrors the requirements for the Notifications data mart. Ultimately, I know that the Data Engineer, Notifications role at Whatnot would allow me to bring my passion for scalable data architecture to the critical work of optimizing high-volume notification event streams. Additionally, my role as a software developer at Haven Technologies enabled me to develop my systems thinking skills. By collaborating closely with product engineers and data scientists to sync data across REST APIs and relational databases, I was able to improve data accuracy by 60% and directly support feature adoption. This background in cross-functional collaboration and production software development allows me to bridge the gap between engineering and analytics effectively. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my data modeling and pipeline architecture skills in working on the Notifications platform at Whatnot. I know that my technical foundation and growth mindset align with what it takes to succeed at the Data Engineer, Notifications role. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
